signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.24; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Implication: risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7169; 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew. Implication: late-trend caution signal as market hedging rather than chasing. Watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3527; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. Implication: as long as BoE holds higher rates, GBP/USD capped but supported. Watch for a break below 1.3500 to shift the read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: EUR LF net short −72, long%=50.0% — flat positioning. HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. EUR/USD at 1.1758; 25d RR at −0.14: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend. Implication: market hedging rather than chasing — late-trend caution, not reversal. Watch for LF delta turning positive or AM reducing to shift the read.
